recmod.tooltip.locked: Use your flash to learn more about this item.
recmod.tooltip.known: Press G to open the log.
recmod.codex.battery.desc: An ordinary battery, the kind you used to buy anywhere. It recharges the camera and, along with it, whatever is left of you. Swapping a battery is the only known way to recover sanity.
recmod.codex.battery.obtain: Found in the chests of old structures and lying on the ground near you. It can also be built from 4 aluminum ingots, 2 gold ingots, glowstone dust and redstone.
recmod.codex.aluminum.desc: Light, soft and dull. It is good for exactly one thing: becoming the shell of a battery. Whoever finds an aluminum vein in a cave is a few steps away from never rationing power again.
recmod.codex.aluminum.obtain: Small veins in any overworld cave, between height -32 and 60. A stone pickaxe is enough. Smelt the raw ore into ingots.
recmod.codex.corrupted_stone.desc: Stone that stopped being stone. It sits colder than the rock around it, and the veins hold a light no mineral has any business holding. It grows in patches in the deep, always as if something underneath had leaked upward and the ground had gone along with it.
recmod.codex.corrupted_stone.obtain: Rare veins in deep caves, below height 40. A stone pickaxe is enough to break it.
recmod.codex.iron_stick.desc: Two bars beaten together until they held as one. It bends where wood would snap, and it carries weight wood could never carry. Everything in this workshop that needs a spine gets this one.
recmod.codex.iron_stick.obtain: Two iron ingots, one above the other.
recmod.codex.hammer.desc: A stone head on a wooden handle. It does exactly one thing, and it wears itself down doing it. No amount of care brings a spent hammer back: you make another one.
recmod.codex.hammer.obtain: Five cobblestone in a helmet shape with two sticks. To use it: hammer on the LEFT slot of an anvil, iron ingots on the right.
recmod.codex.pressed_iron.desc: Iron with the shape beaten out of it. The dents across the face are the tool's signature, and they are the reason no two plates look alike.
recmod.codex.pressed_iron.obtain: Anvil only, never a crafting table. Hammer on the left, iron ingots on the right; each plate costs one point of hammer.
recmod.codex.sharp_scissors.desc: Not the shears from the shed. These were built to take something off a body that had no intention of giving it up. Nothing in this world fits between the blades yet.
recmod.codex.sharp_scissors.obtain: Two pressed iron plates and two iron ingots, crossed.
recmod.codex.black_goo.desc: It is heavier than it looks and it never fully dries. Whatever the corruption is made of, this is what is left once you cook it out of the stone. It does not smell of anything, which is somehow worse.
recmod.codex.black_goo.obtain: Smelt corrupted stone in a furnace. Slow on purpose — there will be a faster way once the Extractor exists.
recmod.codex.corrupted_kit.desc: Stone that stopped being stone, wrapped around a tool that used to be yours. It swings faster and bites harder than diamond, but it will not break obsidian and it will not last. It is a trade, not an upgrade.
recmod.codex.corrupted_kit.obtain: Any diamond tool in the middle of the grid, four corrupted stone around it. Repairs with corrupted stone.
recmod.codex.corrupted_diamond_pickaxe.d: The corruption went into the diamond and the diamond let it in. It breaks anything this world has — and it is the only thing that will take a Tear of Reality off the wall it is hiding in.
recmod.codex.corrupted_diamond_pickaxe.o: A diamond pickaxe in the middle, four black goo in the corners, four emeralds on the sides. Repairs with black goo.
recmod.codex.corrupted_compass.desc: It does not spin. It writes. Hold it up and it tells you, in plain numbers, how far your bed is and which way it lies — and it is never wrong, which is the part that starts to bother you after a while.
recmod.codex.corrupted_compass.obtain: A compass in the middle of the grid, four corrupted stone around it. It is also the seed of larger instruments.
recmod.codex.anchor.desc: Four seconds standing perfectly still, and the world lets go of you. It will not save you from anything that is already running at you — that is the point. It is for the moment you realise how deep you went, while you are still alone down there.
recmod.codex.anchor.obtain: Three iron blocks on top, a corrupted compass between two black goo, three aluminum ingots at the bottom. Sleeps for five minutes after each use.
recmod.codex.lure_clock.desc: It rings where you point it, not where you stand — which is the only reason it is worth carrying. Anything within earshot forgets what it was doing and goes to look. Whatever hunts by sound will care about this far more than a zombie does.
recmod.codex.lure_clock.obtain: Four black goo and three iron rods around an aluminum ingot. Point at a spot up to 32 blocks away and use it.
recmod.tear.sense: You feel something that you don't understand.
recmod.fracture.second: The second cut holds. Something can pass now.
recmod.codex.reality_tear.desc: A shard of the place where the world stopped agreeing with itself. It is warm, it is bright, and it is the only thing you have ever held that does not seem to be entirely here.
recmod.codex.reality_tear.obtain: Deep in caves, hanging in open air, invisible. Take a photo near one and the flash will show it for a few seconds. Only a corrupted diamond pickaxe will take it off the wall.
recmod.codex.fracture.desc:
Two tears and an iron rod, and the blade came out humming. Cut once to open a door, cut again to open its twin, and anything that touches one comes out the other. Crouch and cut to close them both.

Whatever is chasing you can use the door too. That is not a flaw.
recmod.codex.fracture.obtain: A tear of reality above and below an iron rod. Cuts for 8 damage on its own.
